shape.userLocation: object { lat, lon, label } — the resolved user location
shape.lookupResult: object { status, stores, message } — the value section-4 received from section-2's findNearbyLiquorStores
shape.container: DOM element — where section-3 mounts the result UI

shape.rendered: string — one of 'result' | 'empty' | 'error', indicating which state was rendered

notes: section-4 (app shell) passes the user location, the raw lookup result, and a container to section-3. section-3 owns: computing each store's great-circle distance from userLocation, selecting the single closest, and rendering the result card (name, address, distance, directions/map link) OR the empty/error state when lookupResult.status is not 'ok' or stores is empty. The shell does not compute distances or pick the closest store — it only supplies inputs and a mount point. This contract passes data, not logic.
